calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-03-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Latham for and with Vicki Hennessy.

Mr. Latham stated that a little more than $700,000.00 was distributed not to long before the end of the accounting period, that litigation was very contentious, that Ms. Hennessy spent hours and hours with discovery, document production, meeting with Witnesses, that this was not just a routine trust administration and that the one percent formula does not work well with terminating trusts. Ms. Hennessy explained that she did a lot of traveling, that she did a lot of depositions, approved declarations and that there was a lot of property to sell. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-10-2013 Continued To Sep-24-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-27-2013 Continued To Sep-10-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-18-2012 Continued To May-21-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ex Parte Application For Leave To File First Amended Petition
Off Calendar Jan-25-2012 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust And For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-25-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Dubin for Greg More, Paul More and Marcia Blair.

Thomas Latham for Vicki Hennessey.

The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial, (order for the same signed in open court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust And For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-27-2011 Continued To Oct-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Tom Latham for Vicki Hennessy (Trustee). Michael Dubin and Patricia Scott for Greg More.

The Parties stated that they are in agreement to be sent out for trial and that some discovery is still being conducted. The matter was continued to October 25, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and for status of trial setting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust And For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar May-25-2011 Continued To Jul-27-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Lathan for Vicki Hennessey (Respondent). Michael Dubin for Greg Moore, Paul Moore and Marcia Blair.

Counsel stated that discovery is still being conducted. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust and For Removal of Trustee was continued to July 27, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will look into the issue of appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem for Minor Tyler Kelley.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chien.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust And For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-19-2011 Continued To May-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Dubin for Greg More, et al. Thomas Latham for and with Vicki Hennessy.

Mr. Dubin stated that they engaged in some discovery, that depositions were scheduled for the 29th and that a number of Parties still need to be deposed. Mr. Latham indicated that the length of the trial would be two full days and stated that this case was not a good candidate for mediation. The matter was continued to May 25, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and for trial setting. Mr. Dubin stated that he filed a declaration with regard to the dates of deaths of the Brothers and the Sisters of the Couple they are referring to, that he did not know if they died intestate and that provisions of the Trust state that gifts are for the Children of Mary which are his Clients.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Non-Resident Petitioners To File An Undertaking To Secure Costs Including Reasonable Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Feb-16-2011 Denied

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust And For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-08-2011 Continued To Apr-19-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Latham for Vicky Henessey (Trustee). Michael Dubin for Greg More, Paul More and Marsha Blair.

Mr. Latham stated that they were still conducting discovery. The matter was continued to April 19, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of trial setting. The Parties will converse about mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
